opportunities.ai_thesis: SoFi's Mastercard stablecoin deal is going live now, marking a structural inflection point that positions the neobank at the intersection of traditional finance and digital payments infrastructure — a catalyst the market has yet to fully price in. At $18.29, shares sit 44% below their 52-week high with bullish retail sentiment (69% on StockTwits, +0.37 news score) building ahead of institutional recognition, offering an asymmetric entry before momentum accelerates. Muted insider buying reduces conviction on the upside ceiling, but the live partnership catalyst with a score of 100/100 justifies a tactical long here with a near-term target toward the $22–25 range as the deal drives headline flow.

KKR trades 44% below its 52-week high as JPMorgan's credit tightening and software loan markdowns have triggered indiscriminate selling across private credit and PE — but KKR's diversified alternative asset platform and fee-based earnings are far more resilient than the market's 30-40% sector drawdown implies. Neutral-to-bearish sentiment (StockTwits 50% bullish, news sentiment near flat) combined with a 95/100 score signals maximum pessimism has been priced in, historically a high-probability mean-reversion setup. With 13 insider buys logged in 90 days absorbing the selloff, the smart money is accumulating at current levels, making $85 a compelling entry with asymmetric upside as credit conditions stabilize.

POET Technologies is approaching a critical product launch catalyst in ~61 days as its hybrid laser technology for AI data center optical interconnects gains industry recognition, positioning the company to capture share in the high-growth AI optics market as a cost-efficient alternative to incumbents. At $7.98 — 15% below its 52-week high — the stock offers a technically favorable entry with strong retail conviction (95% bullish sentiment, +0.76 news score) before the catalyst fully prices in. With a $1.2B market cap still small relative to the addressable AI infrastructure opportunity, the risk/reward skews bullish ahead of the launch window.

AGI is attracting a wave of institutional conviction with Bank of America, Stifel, and Itau BBA all upgrading or initiating coverage with bullish price targets ranging up to C$80, suggesting 30-60% upside from current levels. At $49.04—12% below its 52-week high—the stock offers a technically attractive entry as it consolidates near support with 100% bullish StockTwits sentiment and strong CAN SLIM fundamentals confirming underlying momentum. The confluence of multiple analyst upgrades, a high composite score of 134, and a discounted entry relative to near-term price targets makes this an actionable BUY now ahead of further institutional accumulation.

The Trade Desk's potential OpenAI advertising partnership represents a transformational catalyst that repositions TTD from a programmatic DSP to an AI-native ad platform, arriving precisely when the stock sits 70% below its 52-week high — offering asymmetric upside at a $13.1B market cap that prices in no partnership premium whatsoever. Strong insider conviction (4 buys in 90 days) signals that insiders are aggressively accumulating ahead of what could be a landmark deal announcement, while 67% bullish retail sentiment confirms the narrative is gaining traction without yet being priced in. Buy TTD at $27.51 with a catalyst trigger on any formal OpenAI partnership announcement, which would likely re-rate the stock toward its historical premium multiple.

Adobe's Q1 earnings report in ~90 days represents a high-conviction catalyst, with the company positioned to deliver its 13th consecutive double beat as generative AI monetization through Firefly accelerates — yet the stock trades 35% below its 52-week high after an "AI disruption" selloff that has priced in an existential threat the fundamentals don't support. At $273.88 with a 97/100 score and 70% bullish retail sentiment, the risk/reward is asymmetric: the market has overcorrected on AI cannibalization fears while Adobe's deeply embedded creative workflow moat and expanding AI feature attach rates remain intact. Enter now before earnings momentum closes the gap to fair value, with negative insider activity the primary risk to monitor.

OXY presents a compelling buy opportunity driven by Wells Fargo's fresh Overweight upgrade on capital efficiency grounds and geopolitical tailwinds — Iranian missile strikes and Strait of Hormuz tensions have pushed oil prices up 5%, directly amplifying OXY's commodity leverage. The entry point is validated now by converging signals: insider buying within the last 90 days, overwhelmingly bullish retail sentiment (StockTwits 94% bullish, +0.49 news score), and a composite score of 116 that clears the buy threshold by a wide margin. With the stock at $56.91 and macro risks actively repricing energy assets higher, initiating a position ahead of any further escalation captures asymmetric upside before the catalyst is fully priced in.

IREN's planned acquisition of 50,000 additional GPUs positions the company to hit $3.7B in AI Cloud ARR by end-2026, a catalyst now 61 days out that the market hasn't fully priced in at 46% below the 52-week high. With StockTwits sentiment at 88% bullish and a composite score of 89/100, retail momentum is building ahead of the expansion announcement, creating an asymmetric entry before institutional attention catches up. Buy here with the $13.6B market cap offering meaningful upside if even a fraction of the ARR target is achieved on schedule.

Arcutis Biotherapeutics (ARQT) offers a compelling risk/reward setup with a Phase 3 ZORYVE cream pediatric atopic dermatitis (ages 2-5) regulatory catalyst expected within ~109 days, backed by already-published positive safety and efficacy data that de-risks the approval pathway. The stock is trading 27% below its 52-week high despite the company having turned profitable and raising 2026 guidance — a rare combination of improving fundamentals and technical compression that signals an attractive entry before the catalyst repricing. Near-zero insider buying is a modest overhang but is offset by 100% bullish retail sentiment and a 121/100 composite score, suggesting the market has not yet fully priced in the expanded pediatric label that could materially grow ZORYVE's addressable market.

Axsome Therapeutics (AXSM) offers a compelling risk/reward setup with the April 30, 2026 PDUFA date for AXS-05 in Alzheimer's agitation — a Priority Review designation that signals FDA confidence in a large, underserved market — just 49 days out, while shares trade 18% below their 52-week high, providing a technically attractive entry before the catalyst. The fundamental backdrop is reinforcing: 66% net revenue growth in 2025 driven by Auvelity and the nascent Symbravo launch demonstrates commercial execution that de-risks the binary event. Despite neutral insider activity, the 80% bullish retail sentiment and near-perfect 99/100 score suggest the market is directionally aligned but hasn't yet fully priced in approval odds, making this a high-conviction asymmetric trade ahead of the catalyst window.

Founder-led board overhaul signals aggressive recognition of operational failures and willingness to install new leadership. With stock down 54% from highs, a new CEO has significant room to stabilize the business and restore investor confidence. Public activism by major shareholder increases accountability and potential for meaningful strategic changes.

events.summary: Travere Therapeutics' FILSPARI (sparsentan) sNDA accepted for Focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS).
Travere Therapeutics has an sNDA accepted for FILSPARI (sparsentan) for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS).
Travere Therapeutics has a PDUFA decision date for FILSPARI (sNDA) for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis treatment.
Travere Therapeutics has a PDUFA date for sNDA of FILSPARI for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis on January 13, 2026.

opportunities.ai_thesis: VERA trades 28% below its 52-week high at $40.60, offering a discounted entry into an FDA catalyst expected within ~117 days (mid-July 2026) before the market has fully priced in the binary event. Neutral sentiment (50% bullish on StockTwits, flat news flow) suggests the stock is in an accumulation lull rather than a crowded trade, historically a favorable setup for pre-catalyst positioning. With a $2.9B market cap, even modest positive FDA read-through could drive a significant re-rating, making the current technical weakness the asymmetric entry point.

ETN is a large-cap industrial player ($137B) with established market position, but lacks near-term catalysts or positive momentum. Current 14% drawdown with insider selling suggests weak near-term conviction.

events.summary: Eaton, an industrial equipment manufacturer, is showing strong technical setups as industrials sector outpaces tech in early 2026.

opportunities.ai_thesis: Stock has pulled back 18% ahead of potential FDA catalyst. If approved, could recover from depressed levels. Market cap of $2.82B suggests established biotech with optionality.
